message:
test(robot): add pure function tests for ensemble/dashboard/sync helpers

Test 10 previously untested functions: synthesisStatus, mergeBudgetConfig,
buildEnsembleHints, resolveEnsembleBudget, yesNo, escapeMarkdownCell,
dashboardCounts, appendUnique, isUnknownJSONFlag, removeFlag. Also
closes bd-p6quz (Robot API ensemble commands fully implemented).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1mlei","title":"Feature: ACFS setup status integration","description":"## Overview
Expose ACFS (flywheel setup) status in NTM to help agents detect missing prerequisites.

## Robot Mode API

```
ntm --robot-acfs-status
```

**Naming Pattern**: Follows tool bridge convention `--robot-<tool>-<action>`

Note: Also aliased as `ntm --robot-setup` for discoverability.

## JSON Output Schema
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "acfs_version": "1.2.3",
  "tools": {
    "br": {"installed": true, "version": "0.8.0", "path": "/usr/local/bin/br"},
    "cc": {"installed": true, "version": "1.0.0", "path": "/usr/local/bin/claude"},
    "cod": {"installed": false, "hint": "npm install -g @openai/codex-cli"}
  }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- NTM can report ACFS presence and version
- Shows which core tools are installed with versions
- Missing tools surface actionable hints","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-1mn8p","title":"Task: DCG (Destructive Command Guard) adapter + robot status","description":"## Overview\nIntegrate Destructive Command Guard (dcg) as a first-class tool.\n\n## Deliverables\n- Add tools.ToolDCG + adapter (detect/version/capabilities/health)\n- Doctor shows dcg status and whether shell guard is active\n- Robot tools report includes dcg\n- Optional: surface dcg status in dashboard (non-blocking)\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- No hard dependency if dcg not installed\n- Health check uses 'dcg status --json' if available","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

-{"id":"bd-1mnb9","title":"Task: Unit tests for Local LLM Support","description":"## Overview
Comprehensive unit tests for the Ollama/local LLM integration.

## Test Coverage Required

### 1. Ollama Adapter Tests (ollama_adapter_test.go)
- TestConnect: Successfully connects to Ollama server
- TestConnect_Unavailable: Handles connection failure gracefully
- TestSendPrompt: Sends prompt and receives response
- TestSendPrompt_Timeout: Handles timeout correctly
- TestStreamResponse: Streaming tokens work
- TestStreamResponse_Cancel: Context cancellation works
- TestListModels: Returns available models
- TestPullModel: Initiates model download
- TestPullModel_Progress: Progress callbacks work

### 2. Mock Ollama Server
```go
type MockOllamaServer struct {
    models    []string
    responses map[string]string
    latency   time.Duration
}

func (m *MockOllamaServer) HandleGenerate(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
    // Simulate streaming response
}
```

### 3. Spawn Integration Tests (spawn_local_test.go)
- TestSpawnLocal_FlagParsing: --local=N parsed correctly
- TestSpawnLocal_ModelFlag: --local-model=X works
- TestSpawnLocal_HostFlag: --local-host=X works
- TestSpawnLocal_MixedFleet: --cc=2 --local=2 works
- TestSpawnLocal_Validation: Invalid model handled
- TestSpawnLocal_OllamaDown: Graceful error when unavailable

### 4. Performance Metrics Tests (metrics_test.go)
- TestTokensPerSecond: Calculation correct
- TestTokensPerSecond_Window: Sliding window works
- TestMemoryUsage: Parses Ollama /api/ps response
- TestLatency: First-token latency tracked
- TestMetricsAggregation: Metrics combine correctly

### 5. Model Management Tests (models_test.go)
- TestListModels: Lists cached models
- TestListModels_Empty: Handles no models
- TestPullModel_Success: Downloads model
- TestPullModel_AlreadyExists: Skips existing
- TestRemoveModel: Deletes model
- TestRecommendModel: Suggests based on VRAM
- TestRecommendModel_NoGPU: CPU-only recommendations

## Test Fixtures
```go
var mockOllamaResponse = `{"model":"codellama:7b","created_at":"2025-01-15T10:00:00Z","response":"func main() {","done":false}
{"model":"codellama:7b","created_at":"2025-01-15T10:00:00Z","response":"\n","done":false}
{"model":"codellama:7b","created_at":"2025-01-15T10:00:00Z","response":"","done":true,"total_duration":1234567890}`
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- 85%+ code coverage
- Mock server enables offline testing
- All tests pass without real Ollama
- Tests run in <10 seconds","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AzureMoose","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:21:20.579646406Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T17:58:44.530612083Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1mnb9","depends_on_id":"bd-1t8p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1mnb9","depends_on_id":"bd-1tb8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1mnb9","depends_on_id":"bd-852e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1mnb9","depends_on_id":"bd-a7wm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1mnb9","depends_on_id":"bd-zcft","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1mxyl","title":"DCG: Add dashboard indicator for DCG status","description":"Add visual indicator in NTM dashboard showing DCG protection status.

## Design

### Location
Top-right status area of dashboard, alongside other status indicators.

### Visual States
- 🛡️ DCG (green): DCG active and protecting
- ⚠️ DCG (yellow): DCG enabled but binary not found
- (none): DCG disabled in config

### Tooltip/Details
When selected, show:
- Commands checked this session
- Commands blocked this session
- Last blocked command (if any)

## Implementation

### Dashboard Model Update
```go
type DashboardModel struct {
    // ... existing fields
    DCGStatus DCGStatusModel
}

type DCGStatusModel struct {
    Enabled   bool
    Available bool
    Checked   int64
    Blocked   int64
    LastBlock *BlockedCommand
}
```

### View Update
Add to dashboard header/status bar:
```go
func (m DashboardModel) View() string {
    // ... existing view code
    dcgIndicator := m.renderDCGIndicator()
    // Insert into status bar
}
```

## Code Location
- internal/tui/dashboard/dcg_indicator.go
- Update: internal/tui/dashboard/model.go

## Styling
Use lipgloss for consistent styling with rest of dashboard","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:45:23.254864594Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T10:20:40.472504211Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T10:20:40.472457132Z","close_reason":"Implemented DCG status indicator badge in dashboard stats bar","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1mxyl","depends_on_id":"bd-1m6sw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1mxyl","depends_on_id":"bd-2q9px","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1mxyl","depends_on_id":"bd-o97j9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1my2","title":"Task: Implement agent capability matrix","description":"## Purpose
Define capability scores per agent type x task type for intelligent assignment.

## Capability Matrix
var agentCapabilities = map[AgentType]map[string]float64{
    AgentClaude: {
        'refactor': 0.95,
        'analysis': 0.90,
        'docs':     0.85,
        'bug':      0.80,
        'feature':  0.85,
        'testing':  0.75,
    },
    AgentCodex: {
        'feature':  0.90,
        'bug':      0.90,
        'testing':  0.85,
        'refactor': 0.75,
        'docs':     0.70,
    },
    AgentGemini: {
        'docs':     0.90,
        'analysis': 0.85,
        'feature':  0.80,
        'testing':  0.80,
    },
}

## Usage
func GetAgentScore(agentType AgentType, taskType string) float64 {
    if learned, ok := learnedScores[agentType][taskType]; ok {
        return learned  // Use learned scores if available
    }
    return agentCapabilities[agentType][taskType]
}

## Acceptance Criteria
- Default scores for all agent/task combinations
- Scores between 0.0 and 1.0
- Configurable overrides
- Integrates with learned scores (Epic #15)

## Technical Notes
Store in internal/assign/capability.go
Allow config overrides","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:43:13.131776968Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T10:08:41.161147162Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T10:08:41.161100564Z","close_reason":"Implemented agent capability matrix in internal/assign/capability.go with tests. Features: default scores, configurable overrides, learned scores support, thread-safe access.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1my2","depends_on_id":"bd-9tbv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3uj4","title":"Task: Install git hooks","description":"## Purpose
Install git hooks for beads sync and Agent Mail guard during project initialization.

## Hooks to Install
1. pre-commit: Run br sync --flush-only to export beads changes
2. post-checkout: Warn about uncommitted bead changes
3. Agent Mail guard: Block commits that conflict with file reservations

## Scope
- Create .git/hooks/ scripts if .git exists
- Make hooks executable (chmod +x)
- Support --no-hooks flag to skip
- Do not overwrite existing hooks (append or warn)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Pre-commit hook runs br sync --flush-only before commit
- Hooks are executable
- Existing hooks not overwritten
- Works with or without Agent Mail

## Technical Notes
Check if .git directory exists before attempting

 {"id":"bd-3ury8","title":"Task: E2E Tests: JFP robot wrapper","description":"## Overview
End-to-end tests for JFP robot wrapper commands with real CLI if available.

## Scenarios
1. `--robot-jfp-list` returns JSON and includes known prompt IDs
2. `--robot-jfp-search` returns hits for a common query
3. `--robot-jfp-show` returns full prompt content
4. `--robot-jfp-install` (dry-run or temp project) installs a skill
5. Missing jfp returns DEPENDENCY_MISSING with hint

## Logging
Use log/slog:
- [E2E-JFP] cmd=<cmd> exit=<code> count=<n>
- [E2E-JFP] error=<err> hint=<hint>

## Acceptance Criteria
- JSON schema stable and parseable
- Commands are non-interactive in robot mode","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:12:12.560054859Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:20:19.802260446Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T22:20:19.802179525Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ury8","depends_on_id":"bd-1edma","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ury8","depends_on_id":"bd-1q6xh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ury8","depends_on_id":"ntm-kbkb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"}]}

NTM already supports webhook notifications. This task **extends** the existing notification system with additional event types (not a new webhook subsystem).

## Scope
- Add event types for: agent-started, bead-assigned, bead-completed, error
- Ensure webhook payload includes session, pane, agent type, and bead ID when applicable

- No separate webhook framework
- No new config files

## Acceptance Criteria
- New events appear in existing notifications pipeline
- Webhook payloads include relevant context fields
- Robot mode outputs can reference notification event IDs when needed
- Unit + E2E tests cover new event types and payload rendering","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T08:41:40.468678597Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T19:35:58.814000946Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-3uydb","title":"Feature: Mode Output Caching","description":"## Summary
Cache mode outputs when context hasn't changed so users can re-run synthesis or compare strategies without re-running expensive analysis.

## Rationale
Caching speeds experimentation and reduces cost when only synthesis strategy changes.

## Implementation
- Cache key includes ContextPack hash + mode_id + mode_version + relevant config hashes (preamble/strategy/model).
- Store serialized ModeOutput in `.ntm/ensemble-cache/` with TTL (default 24h, configurable).
- Invalidate on context change, mode version change, explicit `--no-cache`, or TTL expiry.
- CLI flags: `--use-cache` (default) and `--no-cache`.
- Status output shows hit/miss/invalidations and reason codes.

## Logging
- Log cache hit/miss + invalidation reason (context_hash_changed, ttl_expired, version_bump).

## Tests
- Unit: cache key determinism, TTL expiry, invalidation reasons
- Integration: cache survives restart
- E2E: rerun uses cache; `--no-cache` forces refresh

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Cache key deterministically derived
- [ ] Cached outputs restored correctly
- [ ] Invalidations occur for each trigger
- [ ] CLI flags override cache behavior
- [ ] Logs and status show hit/miss metrics

## Dependencies
- Requires ModeOutput schema (bd-1wnxl)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:06:03.033064607Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T03:50:34.593563036Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T03:50:34.593430209Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3uydb","depends_on_id":"bd-1h4ox","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3uydb","depends_on_id":"bd-1wnxl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3v03r","title":"CAAM: Add dashboard widget for account status","description":"Add dashboard widget showing current account and usage per provider.

## Design

### Widget Location
Side panel or bottom section of dashboard.

### Visual Layout
```
┌─ Accounts ────────────────────┐
│ Claude:  acct1@x.com  [███░░] │
│ OpenAI:  acct2@y.com  [████░] │  <- 80% alert (yellow)
│ Gemini:  acct3@z.com  [█░░░░] │
└───────────────────────────────┘
```

### Features
- Progress bar showing usage/limit
- Color coding: green (<60%), yellow (60-80%), red (>80%)
- Click to expand: show all accounts, switch option
- Cooldown indicator for rate-limited accounts

## Implementation

### Model
```go
type AccountsWidget struct {
    caamManager *caam.Manager
    accounts    []AccountStatus
    width       int
    height      int
}

type AccountStatus struct {
    Provider     string
    Email        string
    UsagePercent int
    InCooldown   bool
    CooldownEnd  time.Time
}
```

### View
Use lipgloss for progress bars and colors:
```go
func (w AccountsWidget) View() string {
    // Render each provider row
    // Include progress bar with color
    // Show alert icon if >80%
}
```

## Code Location
- internal/tui/widgets/accounts.go
- Update: internal/tui/dashboard/layout.go

## Refresh
Poll caam status every 30 seconds (or on account switch event).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:53:15.682385815Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T17:39:51.711753399Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T17:39:51.711702493Z","close_reason":"Implemented accounts.go panel with CAAM status display","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3v03r","depends_on_id":"bd-10qa5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3v03r","depends_on_id":"bd-1vbqd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3v03r","depends_on_id":"bd-3b52y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3v03r","depends_on_id":"bd-8o977","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-p2pr8","title":"Sub-Epic: Critical Low Coverage Packages (<30%)","description":"# Critical Low Coverage Packages

## Scope
Packages with coverage below 30% requiring immediate attention:
- **git**: 2.0% - Git integration utilities
- **gemini**: 19.8% - Gemini CLI agent adapter
- **auth**: 27.4% - Authentication/authorization
- **serve**: 27.6% - HTTP server and API handlers
- **cli**: 27.8% - Command-line interface (largest package)

## Analysis & Approach

### git (2.0%)
- **Why low**: Almost entirely exec-based (runs git commands)
- **Strategy**: Test pure helpers (path parsing, commit message formatting)
- **Target**: 20-30% (limited by external dependency)

### gemini (19.8%)
- **Why low**: Network I/O to Gemini API, state machine logic
- **Strategy**: Test state parsing, response formatting, config validation
- **Target**: 40-50%

### auth (27.4%)
- **Why low**: Browser auth flows, tmux session monitoring
- **Strategy**: Test detection functions, credential parsing
- **Target**: 45-55%

### serve (27.6%)
- **Why low**: HTTP handlers, WebSocket, SSE
- **Strategy**: httptest for handlers, pure helpers already partly tested
- **Target**: 50-60%

### cli (27.8%)
- **Why low**: Massive package (~50 files), mostly cmd execution
- **Strategy**: Test pure helpers (formatting, filtering, validation)
- **Target**: 35-45% (incremental)

## Priority Rationale
These packages handle critical functionality (auth, API, CLI) and low coverage creates regression risk during refactoring.

## Parent
Child of: Epic: Test Coverage Improvement Initiative","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:33:01.816301742Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T05:32:32.833133337Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-p2pr8","depends_on_id":"bd-4b4zf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:40:05.262485737Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":194,"issue_id":"bd-p2pr8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"STRUCTURE (2026-02-05): This sub-epic contains 11 child tasks for packages <30% coverage. CLOSED: git (2%), worktrees (8.1%), tutorial (19.1%), gemini (19.8%), serve (24.3%), cli (24.8%), integrations/pt (26.5%), auth (26.2%), bv (27.0%). OPEN: bd-1tml8 (cli helpers), bd-1iu4x (serve handlers). STRATEGY: Most packages reached ceiling for pure-function testing. Further gains require mocking I/O dependencies. RECOMMENDATION: Focus remaining effort on bd-1tml8/bd-1iu4x open tasks, then close epic.","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:50:38Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-p3en","title":"Task: Define command tier taxonomy and categorization","description":"## Overview
Create the foundational tier taxonomy that categorizes all NTM commands into proficiency levels.

## Deliverables
1. Create internal/cli/tiers/taxonomy.go defining:
   - Tier enum (Apprentice=1, Journeyman=2, Master=3)
   - CommandTier struct with {Command, MinTier, Description, Examples}
   - Global registry of all commands with their tier assignments

2. Tier 1 (Apprentice) - Essential workflow:
   - spawn: Create agent sessions
   - send: Send prompts to agents
   - status: View running sessions
   - kill: Stop sessions
   - help: Get help (tier-aware)

3. Tier 2 (Journeyman) - Full standard:
   - All Tier 1 commands
   - dashboard: Interactive TUI
   - context: View context window usage
   - tail: View agent output
   - interrupt: Send Ctrl+C to agents
   - swarm: Swarm orchestration
   - All standard flags

4. Tier 3 (Master) - Advanced/experimental:
   - All Tier 2 commands
   - --robot-* flags for automation
   - Internal debugging commands
   - Experimental features

## Implementation Notes
- Use Go constants for tier values
- Include help text per tier explaining what unlocks next
- Document promotion criteria in tier definitions

## Acceptance Criteria
- taxonomy.go compiles and exports tier constants
- All existing commands have tier assignments
- Tier progression is logical (each tier builds on previous)
- Unit tests verify taxonomy completeness","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:24:07.875431526Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T06:03:20.700671521Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T06:03:20.700604966Z","close_reason":"Implemented command tier taxonomy in internal/cli/tiers/taxonomy.go with Tier enum, CommandInfo struct, and complete Registry mapping all commands","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-p3en","depends_on_id":"bd-7ny3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:08Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-p6quz","title":"Feature: Robot API - JSON interface for ensemble automation","description":"# Feature: Robot API

## Purpose
Expose ensemble functionality through JSON API for AI-to-AI orchestration and automation pipelines. Follows existing NTM robot mode patterns.

## Scope

### Robot Commands

**--robot-ensemble=SESSION**
Get ensemble state for a session.
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "timestamp": "2026-01-23T10:30:00Z",
  "ensemble": {
    "session": "myproject",
    "question": "What architectural problems exist?",
    "preset": "architecture-review",
    "status": "active",
    "started_at": "2026-01-23T10:25:00Z",
    "modes": [
      {"id": "abductive", "code": "B13", "tier": "advanced", "pane": "myproject__cc_1", "agent_type": "claude", "status": "done"}
    ],
    "synthesis": {"strategy": "deliberative", "status": "not_started"}
  },
  "summary": {"total_modes": 5, "completed": 2, "working": 2, "pending": 1}
}
```

**--robot-ensemble-spawn=SESSION**
Create new ensemble session.
```bash
ntm --robot-ensemble-spawn=myproject \
  --preset=architecture-review \
  --question='What problems exist?' \
  --agents=cc=3,cod=2

ntm --robot-ensemble-spawn=myproject \
  --modes=A1,B13,F43 \
  --allow-advanced=true \
  --question='...'
```

**--robot-ensemble-synthesize=SESSION**
Trigger synthesis.
```bash
ntm --robot-ensemble-synthesize=myproject \
  --strategy=deliberative \
  --format=markdown
```
Returns synthesis output metadata or status.

**--robot-ensemble-modes**
List available modes.
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "modes": [
    {"id": "abductive", "code": "B13", "tier": "advanced", "name": "Abductive Reasoning", "category": {"code": "B", "name": "Ampliative"}}
  ],
  "categories": [{"code": "A", "name": "Formal", "mode_count": 8}],
  "default_tier": "core",
  "total_modes": 80,
  "core_modes": 28,
  "advanced_modes": 52,
  "experimental_modes": 0
}
```

**--robot-ensemble-presets**
List available ensembles (presets).
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "presets": [
    {"name": "architecture-review", "display": "Architecture Review", "description": "...", "modes": [{"id":"systems-thinking","code":"F43"}], "synthesis": {"strategy": "deliberative"}, "budget": {"total": 30000}, "allow_advanced": false, "tags": ["architecture"]}
  ]
}
```

### Modifiers (Unprefixed)
- **Spawn:** `--question`, `--preset`, `--modes`, `--agents`, `--assignment`, `--allow-advanced`, `--budget-total`, `--budget-per-agent`, `--no-cache`, `--no-questions`, `--project`
- **Modes list:** `--category`, `--tier`, `--limit`, `--offset`
- **Synthesize:** `--strategy`, `--format`, `--output`, `--force`

### Strategy Set
Uses canonical set from bd-2ztw9 (manual, adversarial, consensus, creative, analytical, deliberative, prioritized, dialectical, meta-reasoning, voting, argumentation-graph).

### Exit Codes
- 0: Success
- 1: Error (with error_code in JSON)
- 2: Feature unavailable

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All robot commands return valid JSON
- [ ] Follows existing robot envelope pattern (success/timestamp, optional error fields on failure)
- [ ] Exit codes are consistent
- [ ] Arrays initialized to [] not nil
- [ ] Error codes are documented
- [ ] _agent_hints included where helpful

## Technical Notes
- Follow patterns in internal/robot/
- Add to capabilities.go for discoverability
- Ensure envelope compliance (see envelope_test.go)
- Modifiers are **unprefixed** per docs/robot-api-design.md","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:26:15.832578580Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T20:12:58.509021100Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-p6quz","depends_on_id":"bd-12u4u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:08Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-p6quz","depends_on_id":"bd-49fb9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:08Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-pjjk7","title":"EPIC: Canonical Assignment Flow (ntm assign + spawn --assign)","description":"## Goal
Reduce user confusion by providing **one** canonical assignment flow and **one** convenience entry point.

## Decision
- **Keep**: `ntm assign` as the only assignment command.
- **Keep**: `ntm spawn --assign` as the only convenience entry (spawn + assign).
- **Expose**: `--strategy=balanced|speed|quality|dependency|round-robin` on `ntm assign`.
- **Drop/Merge**: `ntm swarm`, `ntm send --distribute`, YAML swarm config, and from‑file assignments.

## Why
Multiple entry points doing the same thing (assign work to agents) multiplies docs, tests, and code paths and will confuse users. The project benefits from a single, well‑designed path.

## Scope
- Keep existing strategies (`balanced`, `speed`, `quality`, `dependency`) intact and documented.
- Add `round‑robin` as an **optional** additional strategy (no renaming of existing ones).
- Implement assignment tracking + reporting so `balanced` can be truly load‑aware.
- Implement `ntm spawn --assign` as a thin wrapper over `ntm assign`.
- Update docs/help to emphasize canonical flow.

## Non‑Goals
- No new orchestration commands outside `assign` + `spawn --assign`.
- No YAML swarm config or additional CLI entry points.

## Success Criteria
- `ntm assign` is the only path for distributing work.
- `ntm spawn --assign` works end‑to‑end with clear output.
- Redundant commands are closed/dropped.
- Strategy list in docs/help matches code.","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-pndtv","title":"Unit Tests: AutoRespawner kill sequences and respawn logic","description":"## Purpose
Comprehensive unit tests for AutoRespawner covering agent-specific kill sequences, respawn cycles, and integration with AccountRotator.

## Test Structure

```go
// internal/swarm/auto_respawner_test.go
package swarm

func TestAutoRespawner_KillSequences(t *testing.T) {
    tests := []struct {
        name      string
        agentType string
        expectedKeys []string
    }{
        {"claude_double_ctrl_c", "cc", []string{"\x03", "\x03"}},
        {"codex_exit_command", "cod", []string{"/exit\n"}},
        {"gemini_escape_ctrl_c", "gmi", []string{"\x1b", "\x03"}},
    }
    
    for _, tt := range tests {
        t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
            mockPaneSpawner := &MockPaneSpawner{t: t}
            respawner := &AutoRespawner{PaneSpawner: mockPaneSpawner}
            
            t.Logf("[TEST] Testing kill sequence for agent type: %s", tt.agentType)
            
            err := respawner.killAgent("test_session:1.0", tt.agentType)
            require.NoError(t, err)
            
            t.Logf("[TEST] Keys sent: %v", mockPaneSpawner.keysSent)
            assert.Equal(t, tt.expectedKeys, mockPaneSpawner.keysSent)
        })
    }
}

func TestAutoRespawner_ForceKillFallback(t *testing.T) {
    mockPaneSpawner := &MockPaneSpawner{
        panePIDs: map[string]int{"test_session:1.0": 12345},
        exitAfter: 3 * time.Second,
    }
    cfg := &config.SwarmConfig{GracefulTimeout: 1 * time.Second}
    respawner := NewAutoRespawner(cfg, mockPaneSpawner, nil, nil)
    
    t.Log("[TEST] Testing force kill fallback")
    err := respawner.killWithFallback(ctx, "test_session:1.0", "cc")
    require.NoError(t, err)
    assert.True(t, mockPaneSpawner.forceKillCalled)
}

func TestAutoRespawner_MaxRetriesAndCooldown(t *testing.T) {
    cfg := &config.SwarmConfig{MaxRespawnsPerPane: 3, RespawnCooldown: 100*time.Millisecond}
    respawner := NewAutoRespawner(cfg, &MockPaneSpawner{}, nil, nil)
    
    pane := "test_session:1.0"
    for i := 0; i < 3; i++ {
        respawner.recordRespawn(pane)
    }
    assert.False(t, respawner.canRespawn(pane), "Should NOT allow after max retries")
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Test kill sequences for all 3 agent types (cc, cod, gmi)
- [ ] Test force kill fallback when graceful fails
- [ ] Test max retries limit enforcement
- [ ] Test cooldown period enforcement
- [ ] Mock PaneSpawner, AccountRotator, PromptInjector

 {"id":"bd-pqwk","title":"Support partial/prefix session names for disambiguation","description":"Support partial/prefix session names for disambiguation.

## Problem Being Solved

Currently you need to type full session name like `vnt destructive_command_guard`. Should support prefix matching.

## Behavior

- If prefix matches exactly one session: use it
- If prefix matches multiple sessions: show matching options, ask user to be more specific
- If prefix matches zero sessions: show error with available sessions

## Examples

```bash
# Currently required
ntm view destructive_command_guard

# Desired
ntm view destr  # works if only one session starts with 'destr'
vnt destr       # same with alias

# Ambiguous
ntm view my
# Error: 'my' matches multiple sessions: my_project, my_test
# Please be more specific.
```

## Implementation

Update session name resolution in all commands:
- ntm view SESSION
- ntm kill SESSION
- ntm send SESSION
- ntm health SESSION
- etc.

## Robot Mode API
Robot mode requires exact names (no fuzzy matching).

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Prefix matching works for all commands taking SESSION arg
2. Exact match preferred over prefix match
3. Multiple matches show list and error
4. Zero matches show available sessions
5. Robot mode requires exact names
6. Works with vnt alias

## Unit Tests (80% coverage target)
- internal/session/resolve_test.go
- Test exact match priority
- Test unique prefix resolution
- Test ambiguous prefix error

## E2E Test Script
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# [E2E-PREFIX] Session Prefix Resolution Tests
LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-PREFIX]"
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Testing prefix session names"
ntm spawn test_session_one --cc=1 --no-prompt
ntm spawn test_session_two --cc=1 --no-prompt
ntm view test_session_o && echo "Prefix worked"
ntm kill test_session_one --force
ntm kill test_session_two --force
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Prefix test complete"
```","acceptance_criteria":"1. Partial session names work for disambiguation
2. Prefix matching finds unique session
3. Multiple matches returns error with suggestions
4. Works with all session-accepting commands
5. Unit tests for matching logic
6. E2E test with multiple sessions","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.
